reasoning: ROM primary source holds under attack. WSGRND.MAC:637 carries the literal comment `;WAVE 1 HAS NO GROUND PHASE`, and the TGDPTR table's first live entry is `TMAP BUNK ... WAVE 02` (638) — there is no WAVE 01 row; VWGRND indexes TGDPTR by GD.WAV (=game_wave-2), so wave 1 has no maze pointer at all. PHIGD:1602 begins `LDA GM.WAV; DECA` and the DECA at WSMAIN 1604 carries the comment `;ALWAYS SKIPPED ON FIRST GAME WAVE` (quoted correctly). So the cabinet's game wave 1 is space->trench with no ground segment — verified, not asserted. Ours (surfaceMazes.ts:251) `if (w === 1) idx = MAZE_ORDER.indexOf('SQUARE')`, and the else-branch `w<=20 -> idx = w-2` maps wave 3 -> idx 1 = MAZE_ORDER[1] = SQUARE. MAZE_ORDER.indexOf('SQUARE') is also 1, so SQUARE is genuinely served at BOTH clone wave 1 and clone wave 3, exactly as the finding states. Divergence is real and the doubling claim is arithmetically correct. Class DIVERGENCE/accept is appropriate: an added segment forced by the clone's uniform per-wave phase machine, not a mis-port.

Attacked on 'is GDBNKGN reachable/shipped' and it held. The BUNKER macro (WSGRND 132-140) emits `.BYTE 3 ;BUNKER` as the object type byte; GDGUN (WSGRND 1187-1201) loads that byte via `LDA TGD$PC(X)`, does `CMPA #PC$BSH` then `LBEQ GDBSHGN` (type 2 = bishop) / `LBHI GDBNKGN` (>2, i.e. type 3 = bunker, cited line 1200 verbatim `LBHI GDBNKGN ;>2==>BUNKER GUN`) / `BRA GDTWRGN` (<2 = tower). GDGUN is called every visible-object frame at WSGRND 779 `JSR GDGUN ;ALLOW BUILDINGS TO FIRE GUNS`, inside VWGRND's per-object loop with NO DEVSYS/test gate — this is shipped play code, and bunkers populate real mazes (BUNK is all-bunker). GDBNKGN (1293) fires with the distance-weighted roll `LDA #40; SUBA 4+M.X0; CMPA P.RND1; IFHS` then exactly one shot: `CMPA #50; IFLO -> JMP FRBFGN` (straight) else `IFMI -> JMP FRBRGN` (right diag) else `JMP FRBLGN` (left diag). Only guard is `BITA #TYP$DM; IFEQ` (guns fire while undamaged) — an undamaged bunker fires. Ours (sim.ts:492) `turret.kind !== 'bunker' && ...` excludes bunkers from `armed`, so bunkers never fire — verified silencing. Finding correct in every particular.

Both directional-gun claims verified against the macro comments and the routines. WSGRND TOWER macro comment is literally `;SHOOTS DIAG AND FORWARDS`, BISHOP is `;SHOOTS DIAG ONLY`. GDTWRGN (1207) fires up to three shots, each on an independent P.RND1 sign roll: `LDA P.RND1; IFMI; JSR FRWFGN` (forward, cited 1239 verbatim), `... JSR FRWRGN` (1243, right diag), `... JSR FRWLGN` (1247, left diag). GDBSHGN (1252) fires FRWRGN (1284) + FRWLGN (1288) only — NO FRWFGN — matching 'diag only'. GDGUN runs per-visible-object per game frame, so density/directionality is geometry-driven exactly as claimed. Ours (sim.ts:485-505): a single global ENEMY_FIRE_INTERVAL cooldown lets ONE random armed object emit ONE fireball whose velocity is `scale(toCockpit(muzzle), ENEMY_SHOT_SPEED)` (cited 500) — a cockpit-homing bolt, not a fixed forward/left/right sparkle vector; and the armed filter `kind !== 'bunker'` lumps tower and bishop together, so the bishop's diag-only rule is unmodeled. Model-level divergence, correctly classed accept (a faithful port entangles the 60-degree FOV). No error found.

Hunted for other exit paths as instructed; the finding survives. PHEGD (WSMAIN 1642) has exactly two ways out: (1) the death exit `LDA S.GAS; LBMI PHIG0D ;J EXIT WHEN DEAD` at the top — but that is the universal shields-depleted death transition, present in every phase, NOT a surface-completion path; (2) the traversal exit `LDA GD.SEQ; CMPA #5 (cited 1679 verbatim `;ONLY GO SO FAR INTO GROUND SEQUENCES`); IFHS ... LDA M$TX+M.S1; CMPA #80; IFLO; LDA #PH$G0B; STA PHASE` — the sole write of PHASE-to-trench, gated on GD.SEQ>=5 AND one more lateral wrap. GD.SEQ increments only in S1MVGD on the signed-overflow wrap of forward travel (WSMAIN 2541 `INC GD.SEQ`), so it is purely a function of distance flown. Critically, killing every tower does NOT write PHASE here: Q.ATP (all-tops-cleared) is referenced at WSMAIN 1316/1630 (init/clear) and 3192/3535 (the '50,000 FOR SHOOTING ALL TOWERS' banner, MS.RWD at 3509) — none inside PHEGD, so the bonus is decoupled from phase length, exactly as claimed. Ours (sim.ts:845) `allTowersKilled(s) || surfaceScrollZ >= surfaceFieldDepth(s.wave)` — the scroll arm mirrors traversal; the allTowersKilled arm ends the surface EARLY, which the ROM does not. Divergence real. (Noted for completeness: the finding's word 'only' is about completion-into-trench; the universal death exit does not undercut it.)

Verified the clamp is the governing code and no shield-penalty path exists for pressing the floor. S1MVGD (WSMAIN 2537, `;GROUND FLYING PLAYER MOVEMENT`, the sole ground-flight movement routine) computes new height into D then `CMPD #1C00; IFGT; LDD #1C00; ELSE; CMPD #200; IFLT; LDD #200; ENDIF; ENDIF; STD M$TZ+M.S1` — a pure two-sided clamp to [GD$MNT=$200=512, GD$MXT=$1C00=7168] (both equated right below at WSMAIN 2596-2597) with NO score/shield/gas side effect; you simply cannot descend past $200. Ground-phase shield loss comes only from object collisions (D-020: `JSR BG1GLW; JSR AUDCR ;AND CRASH INTO TOWER/BUNKER`, WSGRND 911-912/951-952) and turret guns — never from low altitude per se. Ours (sim.ts:457) `if (altitude < MIN_SKIM_ALTITUDE) { damage++; altitude = SKIM_ALTITUDE; events.push({type:'terrain-crash'}) }` charges a shield and bounces the ship — an invented penalty. Finding accurate; $200/$1C00 decimal conversions (512/7168) correct.

Re-did the velocity arithmetic and confirmed $100/$400 are per-game-frame POSITION velocities in raw ROM units, not per-IRQ and not fractional fixed-point. PHIGD:1621 `LDD #100; STD M$VX+M.S1 ;INITIAL PLAYER SPEED` seeds $100=256. PHEGD (main-loop phase handler, not an IRQ — it calls VGTOSITE to pull IRQ rheostat data, so it sits above the IRQ) ramps once per frame at WSMAIN 1660-1662 `LDD M$VX+M.S1; ADDD #1; CMPD #400; IFLS; STD M$VX+M.S1` — +1/frame, capped at $400=1024. That M$VX is added directly to the position accumulator once per frame in S1MVGD (2538 `LDD M$VX+M.S1; ADDD M$TX+M.S1 ... STD M$TX+M.S1`), and M$TX shares the same raw 16-bit unit space as the maze coords (TGD$X/Y, depths to ~$8000) — its signed overflow drives `INC GD.SEQ`. So the units are position-units/game-frame, and the fixed-point interpretation ($100/$4000) is ruled out because the value integrates directly into raw position, not a normalized coordinate. Arithmetic checks: 256 x 20.508 = 5250.0 u/s; 1024 x 20.508 = 20999.9 ~ 21,000 u/s; 5250/600 = 8.75 ~ 9x. GD.SEQ 0->5 = ~5 map-lengths matches PHEGD's CMPA #5 exit (D-019). Ours (state.ts:381) `TURRET_SCROLL_SPEED = 600`, flagged internal tuning, constant, camera-inverted. All ROM numbers and our number verified; no correction needed.
